test_text.title: Inquiry about Apartments in Arilas and Holiday Insurance

audioscript.text: Section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a telephone conversation between a travel company employee and a customer.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example that has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Greek Island Holidays.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, I hope so.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I have a friend who's just come back from Corfu, and she's recommended some apartments in Arilas.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: She thought they might be on your list.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Arilas, Arilas. Let me see, uh, can you give me the names?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, the first's Rose Garden Apartments.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'd like to go with another friend in the last week of October.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, we've got a lovely studio flat available at that time.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'm sure you'd enjoy the entertainment program there too, with Greek dancing in the restaurant.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The entertainment program offers Greek dancing.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So, Greek dancing has been written in the space.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully, and answer questions 1 to 5.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Greek Island Holidays.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, I hope so.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I have a friend who's just come back from Corfu, and she's recommended some apartments in Arilas.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: She thought they might be on your list.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Arilas, Arilas. Let me see, uh can you give me the names?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, the first's Rose Garden Apartments.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'd like to go with another friend in the last week of October.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, we've got a lovely studio flat available at that time.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'm sure you'd enjoy the entertainment program there too, with Greek dancing in the restaurant.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And the cost for each of us?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Two hundred and nineteen pounds.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That sounds very reasonable.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'm just jotting down some notes.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Now the second one she mentioned was called Blue Bay.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Blue Bay.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, in fact that's very popular, and it has some special features.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Really?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The main attraction is the large swimming pool with salt water.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Hmm, much healthier, I understand.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And it isn't far from the beach either, only 300 meters, and only around half a kilometer to some shops, so you don't have to be too energetic.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Is it much more expensive than the first one?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Let me just check.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I think at the time you want to go, it's around 260 pounds - no 275 pounds to be exact.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Right, I've got that.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Now, there are just 2 more apartments to ask you about.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Um. I can't read my own writing.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Something to do with sun... Sunshine, is it?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I think you meant the Sunshade Apartments.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: They're on a mountainside.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Any special features?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Yes, each room has its own sun terrace, and there are shared barbecue facilities.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Oh, sounds lovely.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Yes, it is rather well equipped.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It also provides water sports.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It has its own beach.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There are facilities for water skiing.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Any kite-surfing? My friend's quite keen.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Not at the hotel, but I'm sure you'll find some in Arilas.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There's also satellite TV in the apartments.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And how much is that one?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: £490 with two sharing.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: You mean £245 each?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'm afraid not.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Each person has to pay that amount, and there must be at least two in an apartment.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Oh, I don't think that would be within our budget unfortunately.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And the last one sounds a bit expensive too, the grand.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Actually, it's quite reasonable.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It's an older style house with Greek paintings in every room, and a balcony outside.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Sounds nice.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What are the views like?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, there are forests all round, and they hide a supermarket just down the road.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: So that's very useful for all your shopping needs.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There's a disco in the area too.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And the price?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: 319 pounds at that time.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: But if you leave it till November, it goes down by 40%.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Too late, I'm afraid.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, why don't I send you a brochure with full details, Ms...?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Nash.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: But don't worry about that.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'm coming to Upminster soon, and I'll call and get one.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I just wanted to get an idea first.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, that's fine.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Um. We've got plenty here when you come.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 6 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: If you've got a minute, could I just check a couple of points about insurance?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I got one policy through the post, but I'd like to see if yours is better.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Uh. What would you like to know?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, the one I've got has benefits, and then the maximum amount you can claim.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Is that like yours?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Yes, that's how most of them are.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, the first thing is cancellation.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: If the holiday's canceled on the policy I've got, you can claim £8,000.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We can improve on that, Ms Nash.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: For Greek Island Holidays, our maximum is £10,000.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's good, of course, our holiday won't even cost £1,000 together.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It's still sensible to have good cover.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Now, if you go to hospital, we allow 600 pounds.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, Mine's similar.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And we also allow a relative to travel to your holiday resort.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: My policy just says their representative will help you.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: You can see there's another difference there.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And what happens if you don't get on the plane?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Uh. Nothing as far as I can see on this form.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Don't you have missed departure?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: No, I'll just jot that down.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We pay up to £1,000 for that, depending on the reason, and we're particularly generous about loss of personal belongings up to £3,000 but not more than £500 for a single item.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Then I'd better not take my laptop.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Not unless you insure it separately.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: OK, thanks very much for your time.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: You've really been helpful.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Can I get back to you?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Your name is?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Ben Ludlow.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's LUDLOW.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'm the Assistant Manager here.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'll give you my number.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: But didn't I phone 081260567294, that's what I've got on the paper.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That's the main switchboard.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I've given you my direct line.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Right, thank you very much for your time...
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

body.title: GREEK ISLAND HOLIDAYS

test_text.title: Winridge Forest Railway Park: From Start to Success and Future Plans

audioscript.text: Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a talk on local radio about a children's theme park.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 13.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 13.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: For the second in our series about locally run businesses, we meet Simon Winridge, co-founder of the hugely successful Winridge Forest Railway Park.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Welcome, Simon.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Now perhaps you can begin by telling us a little bit about how it all started.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, during the 1970s my wife Liz and I had just acquired 80 acres of sheep farming land.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And we decided to settle down and have children.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Pretty soon we had a daughter Sarah and a son Duncan.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The place was wonderful for the kids.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: They particularly loved trains, and gradually built up an enormous network of miniature railway track.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I began to develop larger scale models of locomotives, but we didn't think anything more of it until I went on a trip to a theme park near Birmingham, and decided we could do a much better job.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: So we set up a small one ourselves, based on the miniature railway, and we opened to the public for just a month that year, 1984 in July, our driest month.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Because our children said they didn't want our guests to have a miserable wet visit.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I dealt with park business, and Liz carried on with the farmwork.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It soon became clear that we were on to a winner.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We began to extend the railway track and lay it among more interesting landscape by planting trees.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Which in turn attracted more wildlife, and by making cuttings through the rock.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Nowadays we're open all year round, and we're pleased to say that Winridge is one of the most popular visitor attractions in the area, with 50,000 visitors a year.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: A million and a half people have been through our doors since we opened.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 14 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 14 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: All these visitors mean we have had to expand our operation, and it's now a truly family concern.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'm near to retirement age, so I only concern myself with looking after the mechanical side of things.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Keeping the trains going.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Liz now devotes all her energies to recruiting and supporting the large squadron of workers, which keep the place running smoothly.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We're really pleased that after some years away teaching, Sarah has now returned to the park, and makes sure the visitors are kept fed and watered, which keeps her pretty busy, as you can imagine.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Our son Duncan has been a stalwart of the park for the last 10 years, taking over from me in the area of construction.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And I'll say a little bit more about that in a moment.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And his new wife Judith has also joined the team in charge of retail.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's becoming a tremendous growth area for us.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: A lot of people want to buy souvenirs.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: So have you finished your development of the site for the moment?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Not at all.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We are constantly looking for ways to offer more to our visitors.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The railway remains the central feature, and there's now 1.2 km of the line laid, but we'd like to lay more.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Because of the geology of the area, our greatest problem is digging tunnels, but we're gradually overcoming that.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We're also very pleased with a new installation of the Go-Kart arena.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Which is 120 square meters in area.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Again the problem is the geology.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We had to level the mounds on the track for safety reasons.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We wanted to enable 5 to 12 year olds to use the go-karts, and the main attraction here is the Formula 1 Kart.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We've known fights to break out over who gets it, ha ha ha, and then finally to our most recent development, which is the landscaped...
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You will hear a talk on local radio about a children's theme park.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 13.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 13.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: For the second in our series about locally run businesses, we meet Simon Winridge, co-founder of the hugely successful Winridge Forest Railway Park.
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Welcome, Simon.
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Now perhaps you can begin by telling us a little bit about how it all started.
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Well, during the 1970s my wife Liz and I had just acquired 80 acres of sheep farming land.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: And we decided to settle down and have children.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Pretty soon we had a daughter Sarah and a son Duncan.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: The place was wonderful for the kids.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: They particularly loved trains, and gradually built up an enormous network of miniature railway track.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: I began to develop larger scale models of locomotives, but we didn't think anything more of it until I went on a trip to a theme park near Birmingham, and decided we could do a much better job.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: So we set up a small one ourselves, based on the miniature railway, and we opened to the public for just a month that year, 1984 in July, our driest month.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Because our children said they didn't want our guests to have a miserable wet visit.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: I dealt with park business, and Liz carried on with the farmwork.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: It soon became clear that we were on to a winner.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We began to extend the railway track and lay it among more interesting landscape by planting trees.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Which in turn attracted more wildlife, and by making cuttings through the rock.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Nowadays we're open all year round, and we're pleased to say that Winridge is one of the most popular visitor attractions in the area, with 50,000 visitors a year.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: A million and a half people have been through our doors since we opened.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 14 to 20.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Now listen and answer questions 14 to 20.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: All these visitors mean we have had to expand our operation, and it's now a truly family concern.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: I'm near to retirement age, so I only concern myself with looking after the mechanical side of things.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Keeping the trains going.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Liz now devotes all her energies to recruiting and supporting the large squadron of workers, which keep the place running smoothly.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We're really pleased that after some years away teaching, Sarah has now returned to the park, and makes sure the visitors are kept fed and watered, which keeps her pretty busy, as you can imagine.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Our son Duncan has been a stalwart of the park for the last 10 years, taking over from me in the area of construction.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: And I'll say a little bit more about that in a moment.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: And his new wife Judith has also joined the team in charge of retail.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: That's becoming a tremendous growth area for us.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: A lot of people want to buy souvenirs.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: So have you finished your development of the site for the moment?
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Not at all.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We are constantly looking for ways to offer more to our visitors.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: The railway remains the central feature, and there's now 1.2 km of the line laid, but we'd like to lay more.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Because of the geology of the area, our greatest problem is digging tunnels, but we're gradually overcoming that.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We're also very pleased with a new installation of the Go-Kart arena.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Which is 120 square meters in area.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Again the problem is the geology.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We had to level the mounds on the track for safety reasons.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We wanted to enable 5 to 12 year olds to use the go-karts, and the main attraction here is the Formula 1 Kart.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We've known fights to break out over who gets it, ha ha ha, and then finally to our most recent development, which is the landscaped...
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: That is the end of section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to Section 3.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: Simon's idea for a theme park came from
When they started, the family decided to open the park only when
options: the weather was expected to be good.

items.title: Since opening, the park has had

text: What is currently the main area of work of each of the following people?
textReadable: What is currently the main area of work of each of the following people?

test_text.title: Caroline's Dissertation Planning and Study Skills Discussion

audioscript.text: Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a geography student called Caroline discussing her dissertation with her tutor.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 23.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 23.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Ah, Caroline... come on in. Sit down.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Thanks.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: So how's the dissertation planning going?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Ah, Well, Dr Schulmann, I'm still having a lot of trouble deciding on a title.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Well, that's perfectly normal at this stage.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And this is what your tutorials will help you to do.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: What we'll do is jot down some points that might help you in your decision.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: First of all you have chosen your general topic area, haven't you?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, it's the fishing industry.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Oh yes, that was one of the areas you mentioned.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Now uh, what aspects of the course are you good at?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Well, I think I'm coping well with statistics, and I'm never bored by it.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Good.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Anything else?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Well, I found computer modeling fascinating.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: I have no problem following what's being taught, whereas quite a few of my classmates find it difficult.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Well, that's very good.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Do you think these might be areas you could bring into your dissertation?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Oh, yes, if possible.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: It's just that I'm having difficulty thinking how I can do that.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: You see, I feel I don't have sufficient background information.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Uh. Well, do you take notes?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I'm very weak at notetaking.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: My teachers always used to say that.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Well, I think you really need to work on these weaknesses before you go any further.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: What do you suggest?
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the tutorial, you have some time to look at questions 24 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 24 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Well, I can go through the possible strategies with you and let you decide where to go from there.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: OK, thanks.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Well, some people find it helpful to organize peer-group discussions, you know, each week a different person studies a different topic and shares it with the group.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Oh, right.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: It really helps build confidence.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yeah.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: You know, having to present something to others.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I can see that.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: The drawback is that everyone in the group seems to share the same ideas.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: They keep being repeated in all the dissertations.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: OK, you could also try a service called Student Support.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: It's designed to give you a structured program over a number of weeks to develop your skills.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Sounds good.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Yes, unfortunately there are only a few places.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Ah. But it's worth looking into.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, of course.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: I know I've got to work on my study skills.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: And then there are several study skills books you can consult.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: They'll be a good source of reference, but the problem is they are sometimes too general.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, that's what I've found.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Other than that, uh, I would strongly advise quite simple ideas, uh, like using a card index.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Well, yes, I've never done that before.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Uh. It's simple, but it really works because you have to get points down in a small space.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Another thing I always advise is don't just take your notes and forget about them.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Read everything 3 times, that'll really fix them in your mind.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, I can see it'd take discipline, but...
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Well, if you establish good study skills at this stage, they'll be with you all your life.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Oh, yes, I completely agree.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: It's just that I don't seem to be able to discipline myself.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: I need to talk things over.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Well, we'll be continuing these tutorials of course.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Uh. Let's arrange next month's now, let's see.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Um. I can see you virtually any time during the week starting January 22nd.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: What about the 24th?
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: I'm free in the afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Uh. Sorry, I'm booked then.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Uh. What about the following day?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Uh. Thursday?
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Yeah.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I can make the morning.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Fine.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: We'll go for the 25th then.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: That's great.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: Thanks.
audioscript.speaker: CAROLINE

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: An Underground Eco-house Design and Its Features

audioscript.text: Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear part of a lecture about an experimental design for a house.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In the last few lectures, I've been talking about the history of domestic building construction, but today I want to begin looking at some contemporary experimental designs for housing.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So, I'm going to start with a house which is constructed more or less under the ground.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And one of the interesting things about this project is that the owners, both professionals but not architects, wanted to be closely involved, so they decided to manage the project themselves.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Their chief aim was to create somewhere that was as environmentally friendly as possible, but at the same time, they wanted to live somewhere peaceful.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: They'd both grown up in a rural area and disliked urban life.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So the first thing they did was to look for a site, and they found a disused stone quarry in a beautiful area.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The price was relatively low.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And they liked the idea of recycling the land, as it were, as it was, the quarry was an ugly blot on the landscape.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And it wasn't productive any longer either.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: They consulted various architects and looked at a number of designs before finally deciding on one.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As I've said,
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: it was a design for a sort of underground house, and it was built into the earth itself, with two storeys.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The north, east, and west sides were set in the earth.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And only the sloping south-facing side was exposed to the light.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That was made of a double layer of very strong glass.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There were also photovoltaic tiles fixed to the top and bottom of this sloping wall.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: These are tiles that are designed to store energy from the sun.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And the walls had a layer of foam around them too, to increase the insulation.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, what is of interest to us about this project is the features which make the building energy efficient.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Sunlight floods in through the glass wall.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And to maximize it, there are lots of mirrors and windows inside the house.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That helps to spread the light around, so that's the first thing.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Light is utilized as fully as possible.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In addition, the special tiles on the outside convert energy from the sun.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And generate some of the house's electricity.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In fact, and it is possible that in future the house may even generate an electricity surplus.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And that the owners will be able to sell some to the national grid.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As well as that, wherever possible, recycled materials have been used.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: For example, the floors are made of reclaimed wood, and the owners haven't bought a single item of new furniture.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: They just kept what they already had, and then there's the system for dealing with the waste produced in the house.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: This is dealt with organically.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's purified by being filtered through reed beds, which have been planted for that purpose in the garden.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So the occupants of the house won't pollute the land or use any damaging chemicals.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It's true that the actual construction of the house was harmful to the environment, mainly because they had to use massive amounts of concrete.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: One of the biggest sources of carbon dioxide in manufacturing, and as you know, this is very damaging to the environment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In total, the house construction has released 70 tons of carbon dioxide.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Into the air.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now that's a frightening thought.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: However, once the initial 'debt' has been cleared, and it's been calculated that this will only take 15 years, this underground house won't cost anything, environmentally, I mean, because unlike ordinary houses, it is run in a way that is completely environmentally friendly.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So, eco-housing like this is likely to become much more...
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: That is the end of the listening test.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: In the IELTS test, you would now have 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You will hear part of a lecture about an experimental design for a house.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Good morning.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: In the last few lectures, I've been talking about the history of domestic building construction, but today I want to begin looking at some contemporary experimental designs for housing.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So, I'm going to start with a house which is constructed more or less under the ground.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And one of the interesting things about this project is that the owners, both professionals but not architects, wanted to be closely involved, so they decided to manage the project themselves.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Their chief aim was to create somewhere that was as environmentally friendly as possible, but at the same time, they wanted to live somewhere peaceful.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: They'd both grown up in a rural area and disliked urban life.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So the first thing they did was to look for a site, and they found a disused stone quarry in a beautiful area.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The price was relatively low.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And they liked the idea of recycling the land, as it were, as it was, the quarry was an ugly blot on the landscape.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And it wasn't productive any longer either.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: They consulted various architects and looked at a number of designs before finally deciding on one.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: As I've said,
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: it was a design for a sort of underground house, and it was built into the earth itself, with two storeys.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The north, east, and west sides were set in the earth.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And only the sloping south-facing side was exposed to the light.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That was made of a double layer of very strong glass.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There were also photovoltaic tiles fixed to the top and bottom of this sloping wall.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: These are tiles that are designed to store energy from the sun.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And the walls had a layer of foam around them too, to increase the insulation.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Now, what is of interest to us about this project is the features which make the building energy efficient.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Sunlight floods in through the glass wall.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And to maximize it, there are lots of mirrors and windows inside the house.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That helps to spread the light around, so that's the first thing.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Light is utilized as fully as possible.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: In addition, the special tiles on the outside convert energy from the sun.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And generate some of the house's electricity.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: In fact, and it is possible that in future the house may even generate an electricity surplus.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And that the owners will be able to sell some to the national grid.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: As well as that, wherever possible, recycled materials have been used.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: For example, the floors are made of reclaimed wood, and the owners haven't bought a single item of new furniture.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: They just kept what they already had, and then there's the system for dealing with the waste produced in the house.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: This is dealt with organically.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It's purified by being filtered through reed beds, which have been planted for that purpose in the garden.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So the occupants of the house won't pollute the land or use any damaging chemicals.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It's true that the actual construction of the house was harmful to the environment, mainly because they had to use massive amounts of concrete.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: One of the biggest sources of carbon dioxide in manufacturing, and as you know, this is very damaging to the environment.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: In total, the house construction has released 70 tons of carbon dioxide.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Into the air.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Now that's a frightening thought.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: However, once the initial 'debt' has been cleared, and it's been calculated that this will only take 15 years, this underground house won't cost anything, environmentally, I mean, because unlike ordinary houses, it is run in a way that is completely environmentally friendly.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So, eco-housing like this is likely to become much more...
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: That is the end of the listening test.

items.title: The owners of the underground house
options: had no experience of living in a rural area.

items.title: What does the speaker say about the site of the house?
options: It was in a completely unspoilt area.
